title: reset_stalled_portfolio_analytics SECURITY DEFINER lacks REVOKE — cross-tenant data tampering / DoS via any authenticated caller
summary: New SECURITY DEFINER function reset_stalled_portfolio_analytics(INTERVAL) is created with NO REVOKE EXECUTE FROM PUBLIC and NO targeted GRANT. By default Postgres grants EXECUTE to PUBLIC on CREATE FUNCTION, meaning ANY authenticated user (or anon) can invoke it. Because it runs as postgres (SECDEF) it UPDATEs portfolio_analytics across all tenants. The p_stale_threshold is caller-controlled with only a `> 0` check — a caller can pass interval '1 second' to reap virtually all 'computing' rows across every tenant, flipping them to status='failed' with a watchdog error message. This is cross-tenant data tampering plus a DoS against in-flight portfolio analytics computations. The sibling pattern reset_stalled_compute_jobs in mig 20260412094449_compute_jobs_admin_and_defer.sql has the correct REVOKE — this is a clear convention deviation.
evidence: Migration body contains `CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION reset_stalled_portfolio_analytics(p_stale_threshold INTERVAL DEFAULT interval '30 minutes') ... SECURITY DEFINER ... UPDATE portfolio_analytics SET computation_status='failed' ... WHERE computation_status='computing' AND computed_at < (now() - p_stale_threshold);`. grep -c 'GRANT|REVOKE' 20260516122247_portfolio_analytics_stuck_row_reaper.sql returns 0. Reference function reset_stalled_compute_jobs at supabase/migrations/20260412094449_compute_jobs_admin_and_defer.sql contains `REVOKE ALL ON FUNCTION reset_stalled_compute_jobs FROM PUBLIC, anon, authenticated;`.
fix: Append after the COMMENT ON FUNCTION block at L61: `REVOKE ALL ON FUNCTION public.reset_stalled_portfolio_analytics(INTERVAL) FROM PUBLIC, anon, authenticated;` Also consider adding `GRANT EXECUTE ON FUNCTION public.reset_stalled_portfolio_analytics(INTERVAL) TO service_role;` if Railway worker calls via service-role token. Add a self-verifying DO block at file end that calls `PERFORM public._assert_no_public_execute('public.reset_stalled_portfolio_analytics(interval)');` to match the project's standard SECDEF hardening pattern.

title: _assert_strategy_visible_to_allocator orphan-org bypass is more permissive than RLS strategies_org_read — small cross-tenant exposure path
summary: The visibility helper returns TRUE if the strategy's owning org has zero members (orphan org). The comment justifies this as 'avoid frozen scenario commits when the owning org was post-sanitize emptied'. However, this is more permissive than the production RLS predicate strategies_org_read (supabase/migrations/20260409212457_fix_organization_rls_recursion.sql L95-L100) which only admits org-scoped strategies to members of that org via is_org_member — and is_org_member (same file L25-L37) has no orphan-org relaxation. The trigger therefore admits voluntary_add / bridge_recommended INSERTs referencing orphan-org strategies that the calling allocator could NOT SELECT via RLS. Combined with the trigger's RAISE EXCEPTION (L126-L128) echoing the strategy_id back to the caller on failure, this creates a probe path: an allocator can call commit_scenario_batch with an arbitrary strategy_id and learn (a) it exists and is org-scoped, or (b) it exists in an orphan org (INSERT succeeds bypassing browse RLS). Lower severity because the orphan-org condition is rare and post-sanitize narrative is plausible — but the divergence from RLS is a quiet expansion of attack surface that operators should approve explicitly.
evidence: L82-L84: `IF v_org_member_count = 0 THEN RETURN TRUE; END IF;`. Compare to is_org_member at supabase/migrations/20260409212457_fix_organization_rls_recursion.sql L25-L37 which checks `EXISTS (SELECT 1 FROM organization_members WHERE organization_id = org_id AND user_id = auth.uid())` with no orphan-org branch.
fix: Option A (recommended): tighten the helper to match RLS — drop the zero-member fast-path and require allocator membership unconditionally. The post-sanitize orphan scenario is rare enough to warrant a manual admin remediation rather than a permanent code path. Option B: keep the relaxation but add an audit_log emission `log_audit_event_service(allocator_id, 'scenario.commit.orphan_org_bypass', 'strategy', p_strategy_id, jsonb_build_object('org_id', v_org_id))` inside the orphan branch so operators can spot exploitation. Option C: also gate on `strategies.status = 'published'` in the same SELECT (currently the helper does NOT check status — L66-L68 only reads organization_id).

title: _assert_strategy_visible_to_allocator does not check strategies.status — defense-in-depth gap
summary: The visibility helper reads only strategies.organization_id (L66-L68). It does NOT check strategies.status. The comment at L64-L65 says 'globally visible while published' but the code does not enforce 'while published'. The upstream commit_scenario_batch RPC does separately check v_strategy_status='published' (mig 082 L153-L159 + bridge_recommended branch), so the gap is currently closed by the RPC. But the trigger is presented as a defense-in-depth gate that fires 'regardless of which RPC or route is inserting' (L13-L15 comment). A future writer that forgets the status check would bypass status enforcement at the trigger. Lower severity because match_decisions INSERT is gated by RLS to admin + service_role, so casual code paths can't bypass.
evidence: L66-L68: `SELECT organization_id INTO v_org_id FROM strategies WHERE id = p_strategy_id;` — no status column read, no status filter. Helper returns TRUE for any organization_id IS NULL strategy regardless of status.
fix: Extend the SELECT to read status: `SELECT organization_id, status INTO v_org_id, v_status FROM strategies WHERE id = p_strategy_id;` and add `IF v_status IS DISTINCT FROM 'published' THEN RETURN FALSE; END IF;` after the not-found short-circuit. The 'archived' / 'draft' / 'pending_review' shapes should never pass visibility for non-owners.

title: _match_decisions_visibility_check trigger error message confirms strategy existence to non-member allocator (small enumeration leak)
summary: On visibility failure the trigger raises 42501 with `strategy % is not visible to allocator % (org-scoped, allocator not a member)`. This echoes back the user-supplied strategy_id but ALSO confirms the strategy exists and is org-scoped (vs nonexistent — which short-circuits at the helper's IF v_org_id IS NULL returning TRUE). An attacker that fuzzes UUIDs through commit_scenario_batch can distinguish 'strategy exists but not visible' (this error) from 'strategy doesn't exist' (different downstream RPC error from IF v_strategy_status IS NULL). This is a tiny enumeration oracle. Existing strategies_org_read RLS already lets users probe existence via SELECT 404 vs 200, so the marginal disclosure is small — but for an explicit hardening migration it's worth tightening.
evidence: L126-L130: `RAISE EXCEPTION 'match_decisions visibility check: strategy % is not visible to allocator % (org-scoped, allocator not a member). audit-2026-05-07 M-0825.', NEW.strategy_id, NEW.allocator_id USING ERRCODE = 'insufficient_privilege';`
fix: Replace the org-specific message with a generic one: `RAISE EXCEPTION 'match_decisions: strategy not visible to allocator' USING ERRCODE = 'insufficient_privilege';` — log the strategy_id + allocator_id + reason via PERFORM public.log_audit_event_service(...) before the RAISE so operators retain forensic evidence without leaking to the client.

title: Non-SECDEF helpers (_assert_retention_columns, _validate_scenario_diff, _match_decisions_visibility_check) lack SET search_path — minor convention drift
summary: The `_assert_retention_columns` helper is LANGUAGE plpgsql but lacks `SET search_path = public, pg_catalog`. Same pattern in `_validate_scenario_diff` (mig 20260516160800 L49-L56) and `_match_decisions_visibility_check` (mig 20260516160700 L114-L117). None of these are SECURITY DEFINER so the bypass risk is low (search_path manipulation only affects SECDEF privilege boundary). However the project convention across 89 prior migrations is to lock search_path explicitly on every migration-installed function. Informational — call out for convention parity. Not exploitable because none of these have SECDEF, no dynamic SQL, and most are REVOKEd from app roles.
evidence: L40-L43 of mig 20260516160200: `CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public._assert_retention_columns() RETURNS VOID LANGUAGE plpgsql AS $fn$` — no SET search_path. Same shape in mig 20260516160800 L49-L56 (_validate_scenario_diff) and mig 20260516160700 L114-L117 (_match_decisions_visibility_check). Compare to sanitize_user mig 20260516160100 L41-L42 which uses LANGUAGE plpgsql SECURITY DEFINER SET search_path = public, pg_catalog.
fix: Add `SET search_path = public, pg_catalog` between LANGUAGE plpgsql and AS $fn$/$trg$ in all three function definitions. Cheap convention parity that aids future audits.
